articles.name: SSO errors: This page doesn't exist and Access denied
articles.title: SSO errors: This page doesn't exist and Access denied
articles.body:
Issue symptoms

When end users or agents try to sign in through an SSO provider, they are redirected to one of the following pages:


Resolution steps

This page doesn't exist

If agents see this error, team member SSO might not have been configured correctly, or the return URL is not redirecting the agent to the agent dashboard. 

If the end user seeing this error is authenticating through SSO, the help center is not enabled for end users. Activate the help center to rectify this behavior.

Access denied
If agents see this error, team member SSO might not have been configured correctly, or the agent wasn't authenticated correctly through SSO. 

If end users see this error, they are being redirected to the agent dashboard instead of help center. Share the link to your help center with your end users instead.

For more information, see these articles:

  • Single sign-on (SSO) options in Zendesk
  • Enabling and activating your help center
THIS SECTION IS AI CONTENT. DON'T EDIT OR DELETE.

Can an agent see "This page doesn't exist" when signing in through Single Sign-On? Does an incorrect SSO configuration redirect a support person to "This page doesn't exist"? Can a team member experience a "This page doesn't exist" error during Login with Google/Okta?

You did not configure team member SSO or Single Sign-On correctly, or the return URL does not redirect the agent or support person to the agent dashboard or inbox.

Can a customer see "This page doesn't exist" when authenticating through Single Sign-On? Does an inactive knowledge base cause a "This page doesn't exist" error for a user during SSO login? Can a client get a "This page doesn't exist" error when using Login with Google/Okta?

The system displays this error when you do not enable the help center or knowledge base for end users or customers.

  1. Activate the help center or knowledge base.

This action rectifies the behavior for your users.

Can a team member see "Access denied" when signing in through Single Sign-On? Does an incorrect SSO configuration cause an "Access denied" error for a support person? Can an agent experience an "Access denied" error during Login with Google/Okta?

You did not configure team member SSO or Single Sign-On correctly, or the system did not authenticate the agent or support person correctly through SSO.

Can a customer receive an "Access denied" error when logging in with Single Sign-On? Does the system redirect a user to the inbox and show "Access denied" during SSO? Can a client see "Access denied" if they use Login with Google/Okta?

The system redirects end users or customers to the agent dashboard or inbox instead of the help center or knowledge base.

  1. Share the link to your help center or knowledge base with your end users or customers.

This step ensures they access the correct page.

articles.name: Why was I charged  $1 for Voice usage?
articles.title: Why was I charged  $1 for Voice usage?
articles.body:
Question

I am on Voice Lite or did not use Zendesk Voice (formerly Talk) this month. Why was I still charged $1 for usage?

Answer

The cost for the Voice Lite subscription is $0 per agent and Voice trials are at no cost. However, if you have an active phone number on the account, you are charged each month based on the number.

When a free Suite trial starts, you are automatically assigned a Voice number for the trial period. If you plan to subscribe to Zendesk but do not wish to use Voice, you must remove the number to avoid the usage costs.

The price of a phone number depends on the country from where it was purchased. For example, local numbers from the US and Canada cost $1, while toll-free numbers cost $2. For a full list of prices by country, see Zendesk Voice number availability and pricing.

If you want to delete your Voice number and not receive this monthly charge, see Managing Zendesk Voice numbers.

THIS SECTION IS AI CONTENT. DON'T EDIT OR DELETE.

Concept and Confirmation

Why am I charged for Voice Lite? Why is there a charge for Zendesk Voice or Talk? Is Zendesk Voice Lite free? Do Voice trials cost money?

The Voice Lite subscription or license costs $0 per agent and Voice trials cost nothing. If you keep an active phone number on the account or instance, Zendesk charges you each month based on that number.

Scope and limitations

How much does a Zendesk Voice or Talk phone number cost? What is the price of a local or toll-free number? How much do United States or Canada numbers cost?

The price of a phone number depends on the country where you purchase it. Local numbers from the United States or Canada cost $1, while toll-free numbers cost $2. For a full list of prices by country, see Zendesk Voice number availability and pricing.

Does the free Suite trial include a phone number? Does Zendesk automatically assign a Voice number during a trial?

When a free Suite trial starts, Zendesk automatically assigns a Voice or Talk number to your account or instance for the trial period.

Pro tip

How do I avoid Zendesk Voice or Talk charges? How do I stop the monthly fee for a phone number? How do I delete a Voice number?

If you plan to subscribe to Zendesk but do not wish to use Voice or Talk, you must remove the number to avoid usage costs. To delete your number and stop this monthly charge, follow this step:

  1. Remove the phone number from your account or instance.

For more details, see Managing Zendesk Voice numbers.

articles.name: Troubleshooting omnichannel routing issues
articles.title: Troubleshooting omnichannel routing issues
articles.body:
If you have problems with your omnichannel routing setup, use this guide to find solutions to common issues.

This article covers the following topics:

  • Review Copilot recommendations
  • Omnichannel routing settings not visible in Admin Center
  • Abandoned call tickets
  • Agents get more tickets than their capacity limits
  • Voice tickets don't route to the right groups
  • Messaging tickets don't route correctly
  • Unavailable settings and functions
  • Expected behaviors

Review Copilot recommendations

If you have the Copilot add-on, review artificial intelligence (AI) recommendations about your omnichannel routing settings. Copilot flags routing issues and provides systemic recommendations to optimize your operations. For more details, see Optimizing your account with admin copilot insights and recommendations.

Omnichannel routing settings not visible in Admin Center

If settings for omnichannel routing don't appear in Admin Center, check whether you meet the requirements.

Abandoned call tickets

Abandoned call tickets might appear in your queue with omnichannel routing on. This happens because omnichannel routing creates tickets for calls but doesn't support the Create tickets for abandoned calls setting. For a workaround, see Automatically closing tickets created by abandoned calls.

Skills routing doesn't assign to tickets with omnichannel routing

If skills routing doesn't work, you're likely not using triggers or your trigger conditions aren't set correctly.

To ensure skills routing works:

  1. Verify that the skill applies to your tickets
  2. Use triggers to assign skills to tickets because they can add skills when tickets are updated as well as when tickets are created
  3. The skills actions for ticket triggers also let you specify the skill priority for routing
  4. Skills marked as Required can't time out and are part of the criteria for routing the ticket to an agent, but optional skills stop being considered for routing when a skills timeout occurs
  5. Trigger actions related to skills are available when you've created at least one skill

Agents get more tickets than their capacity limits

Agents may receive more tickets than their allowed capacity. For more information about why this happens, see My agent automatically gets assigned more tickets than their capacity allows.

Voice tickets don't route to the right groups

When omnichannel routing is off, Voice (formerly Talk) creates tickets after an agent accepts a call. When it's on, the system creates tickets immediately and routes them to available agents before they accept the call. Triggers that run on these Voice tickets can affect routing. Update triggers to exclude Voice channel tickets. Use this condition in your trigger: Ticket > Channel | Is not | Phone call (incoming).

If you use generative AI to transcribe and summarize your calls and also have intelligent triage on, sentiment detection may assign an intent value that routes the ticket based on a business rule. Check the events of the ticket to confirm this.

Messaging tickets don't route correctly

If you use custom omnichannel routing queues, messaging tickets route automatically.

If you use the standard omnichannel routing queue, you must assign tickets to a group before they're eligible for omnichannel routing. Adjust or create triggers to assign groups to messaging tickets. For more information, see Requirements for the routing trigger.

A trigger action called Set routing channel lets you change a messaging ticket's routing channel to email. When you change the routing channel from messaging to email, add the auto-routing tag to the ticket. Don't use only the Ticket > End session action in the trigger. This is incompatible with the Set routing channel action, which ends the session and changes the routing channel. After you change the routing channel and add the auto-routing tag, omnichannel routing treats tickets as email tickets and they count toward the agent's email capacity instead of messaging capacity. This manages queue eligibility and balances workloads across channels. For more information, see About ending messaging sessions.

Unavailable settings and functions

Omnichannel routing offers a unique experience, but some settings aren't available. Compare your missing settings to the known limitations in Requirements and limitations of omnichannel routing.

Note: Omnichannel routing is the default routing experience for all new and trial customers of Zendesk Suite created on or after December 11, 2024.

Tip: If you created custom omnichannel queues, set up a dashboard for live data on routing performance with this recipe Reporting on custom omnichannel queue performance.

Expected behaviors

  • Round robin treats offers as assignments. This results in an uneven distribution if agents don't accept.

    If there's a large discrepancy among some agents, interview your agents to understand why they don't accept the offers they receive.

  • Tickets leave a custom queue after a ticket update if they no longer meet the queue conditions.

    Ensure the conditions of the desired queue meet the ticket features for every expected subsequent ticket update. For example, remove condition Ticket is created.

  • Updates in the queue setup don't apply to tickets that are already in the queue.

    Tickets follow the queue configuration at the time they joined it.

  • Omnichannel routing (OCR) offers newer tickets first if service level agreement (SLA) prioritization is active.

    Ensure tickets have an active target at all times, deactivate SLA prioritization, or use custom queues. Custom queues priority takes precedence over SLAs. This lets older tickets join a higher priority queue based on the use case.

  • OCR only routes tickets without an assignee.

    If a group contains only one agent, a system ticket rule assigns the ticket to that agent when the ticket lands in the group. Review the ticket events to confirm if this event took place immediately after ticket creation.

  • OCR routes tickets unevenly among agents due to higher priority skills.

    Ensure an admin assigns the correct skills to all agents.

  • Brand memberships prevent assignments.

    Ensure an admin assigns the correct brands to all agents.

  • OCR doesn't offer Voice and messaging tickets to secondary groups of a custom queue if an agent is available in the primary group but doesn't accept the ticket.

    Agents must set themselves to Away or Offline whenever they aren't available to take tickets.

  • OCR doesn't assign reopened tickets to agents with Online status.

    OCR reassigns reopened tickets based on the agent status of the current assignee. Ensure you select the agent status that the reopened ticket becomes unassigned from.

THIS SECTION IS AI CONTENT. DON'T EDIT OR DELETE.

What is the admin Copilot or AI Assistant add-on? Why should I use Copilot insights for omnichannel routing? How does Agent assist optimize operations?

The Copilot or AI Assistant add-on flags routing issues and provides systemic recommendations to optimize your operations. You use these artificial intelligence or AI recommendations to review your omnichannel routing settings.

Why are omnichannel routing settings not visible in Admin Center? Why can I not see my routing settings? What are the requirements for omnichannel routing visibility?

If settings for omnichannel routing do not appear in Admin Center, you do not meet the requirements. Check your account configuration to verify your eligibility.

Why do abandoned call tickets appear in my queue? Why does omnichannel routing create tickets for abandoned phone calls? Can I turn off tickets for abandoned calls?

Abandoned call tickets appear in your queue because omnichannel routing creates tickets for calls but does not support the Create tickets for abandoned calls setting. You can use a workaround to close these tickets.

How do I fix skills routing when it does not assign tickets? Why does skills routing not work with omnichannel routing? How do I use triggers to assign skills to tickets?

If skills routing does not work, you do not use triggers or notification rules, or your trigger conditions are incorrect. To ensure skills routing works, follow these steps:

  1. Verify that the skill applies to your tickets.
  2. Use triggers or notification rules to add skills when you create or update tickets.
  3. Specify the skill priority for ticket assignment in the trigger actions.
  4. Mark skills as Required so they do not time out and remain part of the criteria to route the ticket to an agent or support person. Optional skills no longer apply to routing when a skills timeout occurs.
  5. Create at least one skill to make trigger actions related to skills available.

Why do agents get more tickets than their capacity limits? Why does my team automatically receive tickets beyond their allowed capacity? Can an agent exceed ticket capacity limits?

Agents or team members can receive more tickets than their allowed capacity due to specific system behaviors. Review the designated resource guide to understand why this automatic assignment happens.

Why do Voice tickets not route to the right groups or departments? How do I update triggers for Phone call tickets? How do I prevent Voice channels from routing incorrectly?

When omnichannel routing is active, the system creates tickets immediately and routes them to available agents or support persons before they accept the call. Triggers or notification rules that run on these Voice tickets can affect assignment. Update your triggers to exclude Voice channel tickets by using this condition: Ticket Channel | Is not | Phone call (incoming).

Does generative AI affect how tickets route to my team? Can sentiment detection change ticket routing? How does intelligent triage interact with business rules?

Yes. If you use generative AI to transcribe and summarize your calls and you also activate intelligent triage, sentiment or customer mood detection can assign an intent or customer goal value. This value routes the ticket based on a business rule. Check the events of the ticket to confirm this action.

Why do messaging tickets not route correctly? How do I route messaging tickets using a standard omnichannel routing queue? How do I change a messaging ticket to an email ticket?

Custom omnichannel routing queues route messaging tickets automatically. If you use the standard queue, you must assign tickets to a group or department before they qualify for routing. Adjust or create triggers to manage this process using these guidelines:

  1. Use the trigger action Set routing channel to change a messaging ticket's routing channel to email.
  2. Add the auto-routing tag to the ticket when you change the channel.
  3. Do not use only the Ticket End session action in the trigger, as it is incompatible with Set routing channel.

After you change the channel and add the tag, the system treats tickets as email tickets. They then count toward the email capacity of the agent or support person instead of messaging capacity to balance workloads.

What settings are unavailable in omnichannel routing? Is omnichannel routing the default experience for new accounts? What are the limitations of omnichannel routing?

Some settings are unavailable in omnichannel routing. You can compare unavailable settings to the known limitations documented in the requirements. Omnichannel routing is the default routing experience for all new and trial customers or clients of Zendesk Suite created on or after December 11, 2024.

How do I build a dashboard for live data on routing performance? How do I report on custom omnichannel queue performance? Can I see real-time analytics for custom queues?

If you create custom omnichannel queues, you can set up a dashboard or inbox view for live data on routing performance. Use the designated analytics recipe named Reporting on custom omnichannel queue performance to build it.

Why does round robin distribute tickets unevenly? Why do some agents receive more ticket offers than others? How does round robin assignment work?

Round robin treats offers as assignments, which results in an uneven distribution if agents or team members do not accept them. If a large discrepancy exists among your team, interview your support staff or team members to understand why they do not accept the offers they receive.

Why do tickets leave a custom queue after an update? Do queue setup updates apply to existing tickets? How do queue conditions affect ticket updates?

Tickets leave a custom queue after a ticket update if they no longer meet the queue conditions. Ensure the conditions of your desired queue meet the ticket features for every subsequent update, such as when you remove the condition Ticket is created. Additionally, updates in the queue setup do not apply to tickets already in the queue, as tickets follow the configuration from the moment they join.

Does omnichannel routing prioritize newer tickets over older tickets? How do SLA deadlines affect ticket routing priority? How do custom queues interact with SLAs?

Omnichannel routing offers newer tickets first if service level agreement or SLA prioritization is active. To manage this behavior, ensure tickets maintain an active target at all times, deactivate SLA prioritization, or use custom queues. Custom queue priority takes precedence over due dates or deadlines, which allows older tickets to join a higher priority queue based on your use case.

Does omnichannel routing assign tickets that already have an owner? Why did a ticket automatically assign to a specific agent upon creation? Does routing apply to assigned tickets?

No. Omnichannel routing only routes tickets without an assignee or support person. If a group or department contains only one agent, a system ticket rule assigns the ticket to that agent or team member when the ticket lands in the group. You can review the ticket events to confirm if this action occurred right after ticket creation.

Why does omnichannel routing distribute tickets unevenly based on skills? Do brand memberships prevent ticket assignments? Why are tickets distributed unevenly among my agents?

Omnichannel routing routes tickets unevenly among agents or team members due to higher priority skills. To fix this, an admin must assign the correct skills to all support staff. Furthermore, brand or multiple websites memberships prevent assignments, so an admin must ensure they assign the correct brands or accounts to all agents.

Why does omnichannel routing not offer tickets to secondary groups? How does agent status affect reopened tickets? Does routing assign reopened tickets to online agents?

Omnichannel routing does not offer Voice and messaging tickets to secondary groups of a custom queue if an agent or team member is available in the primary group or department but does not accept the ticket. Agents must set their status to Away or Offline whenever they are unavailable to take tickets. Additionally, the system does not assign reopened tickets to agents with Online status; instead, it reassigns them based on the status of the current assignee. Ensure you select the agent status that the reopened ticket becomes unassigned from.

articles.name: How can I find changes to specific users with the audit log?
articles.title: How can I find changes to specific users with the audit log?
articles.body:
Question

How can I find changes to specific users with the audit log?

Answer

On Enterprise plans, you can see changes to specific users in your audit log. Audit logs serve as a powerful troubleshooting tool, allowing you to track changes made to a specific user and the exact times these changes occurred. By utilizing audit logs, you can effectively monitor user activity and identify any modifications that may impact performance or security.

To find changes related to a specific user, open the audit log and use the following settings in the audit log filter. See Filtering the audit log for details.

  • Activity type = Updated
  • Item > Type = Customer for end-user role or Team member for agent or admin roles
  • Item > Names = The name of the user

Audit logs are also available from the API. You can use the API request to retrieve audit logs. For example the following API request: https://yoursubdomain.zendesk.com/api/v2/audit_logs?filter[source_type]=user&filter[source_id]=379921250014

The API request returns JSON results with details such as the following:

  • Source type
  • Source ID
  • Action performed
  • Timestamp of the action
  • User who performed the action

For more information, see the article: Using audit logs to track activity.

Note these important implications:
  • Some user changes, such as suspension, have a unique Type value and will not be returned by the above methods. User suspension events will have a Type value of User Setting, but the ID is no longer the user's ID.
  • Updates to custom fields are not supported in the audit logs
THIS SECTION IS AI CONTENT. DON'T EDIT OR DELETE.

What is the audit log? Why should I use audit logs to monitor user activity? How do audit logs help troubleshoot changes to a customer or team member?

Audit logs serve as a powerful troubleshooting tool that tracks changes made to a specific customer or team member and the exact times these changes occur. You use them to monitor user or client activity and identify any modifications that impact performance or security.

Can I find changes to specific users with the audit log? How do I track modifications to a customer or team member? How do I filter the audit log for a specific user?

Yes, you can track changes to specific users or clients in your audit log. To find changes related to a specific user, open the audit log and use the following settings in the audit log filter:

  1. Activity type = Updated
  2. Item Type = Customer for an end-user role or Team member for an agent or admin role
  3. Item Names = The name of the user

 

Can I retrieve audit logs from the API? How do I connect to other apps or push data to get audit logs? How do I request audit logs for a specific user ID?

Yes, audit logs are also available from the API to connect to other apps or push data. You can use the API request to retrieve audit logs, for example the following request: https://yoursubdomain.zendesk.com/api/v2/audit_logs?filter[source_type]=user&filter[source_id]=379921250014. The API request returns JSON results with details such as the Source type, Source ID, Action performed, Timestamp of the action, and User who performed the action.

What are the limitations of the audit log? Which plan type or pricing tier includes user audit logs? Can I track updates to a custom field or extra dropdown?

You can see changes to specific users in your audit log only on Enterprise plans or suite versions. Note these important implications and boundaries: some user changes, such as suspension, have a unique Type value and the regular methods do not return them. User suspension events have a Type value of User Setting, but the ID is no longer the user's ID. The audit logs do not support updates to a custom field or extra dropdown.

articles.name: Why aren't SLA badges or targets appearing?
articles.title: Why aren't SLA badges or targets appearing?
articles.body:
Question

I created SLA policies for my tickets, but I don't see an SLA badge or my SLA target. What is going wrong?

Answer

There are several reasons why an SLA badge may not appear, which are related to SLA targets and ticket details. See some examples of the most common reasons below:

  • Many SLA targets don't work on tickets where the agent is the requester
  • The ticket doesn't have an active target applied or the ticket doesn't meet the SLA policy's conditions
  • The ticket doesn't have a priority set. SLAs require that a ticket has a priority set to know what time to use for the countdown.
  • The ticket response is a private comment. Private comments don't activate reply targets.
  • The ticket met the SLA target before the SLA policy was applied, so the SLA target doesn't appear
Note: SLAs don't apply to tickets that are solved upon creation. The solved status satisfies SLAs and prevents policies to apply. For live chat, admins can set up First and Next reply time SLAs.
 
THIS SECTION IS AI CONTENT. DON'T EDIT OR DELETE.

Why aren't SLA badges or targets appearing? Why is my due date or deadline missing from a ticket? Why can I not see the SLA target countdown?

Your SLA badge or target does not appear for several reasons related to targets and ticket details:

- Many SLA targets do not work on tickets where the agent or support person is the requester or customer.

- The ticket does not have an active target applied or does not meet the conditions of the SLA policy.

- The ticket does not have a **Priority** set. SLAs require a **Priority** to determine the countdown time.

- The ticket response is a **Private comment** or internal note. Private comments do not activate reply targets.

- The ticket met the SLA target before the system applied the SLA policy.

Note that SLAs do not apply to tickets with a **Solved** ticket status upon creation because the solved state satisfies SLAs. For live chat, admins can set up **First reply time** and **Next reply time** SLAs.

articles.name: Error: "View only. You need to have Chat enabled to reply to this conversation."
articles.title: Error: "View only. You need to have Chat enabled to reply to this conversation."
articles.body:
Issue symptoms

When I attempt to comment on a ticket I receive this error: View only. You need to have Chat enabled to reply to this conversation. 

Resolution steps

This error occurs when the user attempting to comment on the ticket doesn't have a Chat license. If you're an admin, you can fix this issue yourself. If you are not an admin, contact your team so that you can be given the correct access.

For steps on how to grant product access to an agent, see this article: How do I grant an agent access to a product? [Video]

For more information, see these articles:

  • How do I verify that agents who receive “Zendesk Chat is not enabled for you” have the correct permissions?
  • How can I resolve the error message "Failed to add team member. Please try again" when adding an agent to chat?
THIS SECTION IS AI CONTENT. DON'T EDIT OR DELETE.

Why do I see "View only. You need to have Chat enabled to reply to this conversation" when replying to a ticket? Why can't an agent or team member comment on a ticket? Why is a ticket view only or read only?

This issue occurs because the agent or support person attempting to comment on the ticket does not have a Chat seat or license. An administrator must grant the correct product access or permissions to resolve this error.

How do I fix the error "View only. You need to have Chat enabled to reply to this conversation" as an admin? How do I grant an agent or support person access to Chat?

  1. Review the access level and permissions of the affected team member.
  2. Grant product access to the agent so they receive a Chat license.

What should I do if I am not an admin and get a view only error? How can a team member or agent resolve a missing Chat license error without admin permissions?

Contact your team or administrator so they can grant you the correct access and assign a Chat seat to your account.

articles.name: Why do I receive the error: DNS records are not set up correctly?
articles.title: Why do I receive the error: DNS records are not set up correctly?
articles.body:
Question

I am trying to allow Zendesk to send an email on behalf of my domain, but I receive the error DNS records are not set up correctly when verifying my DNS records. Why does this error appear?

Answer

The error DNS records are not set up correctly appears when Zendesk is unable to verify your DNS records. This is often caused by DNS records that are either unpublished or published incorrectly. This article outlines how to determine possible issues when your DNS records do not validate and information on how to resolve it.

To ensure your DNS records are correctly configured and that Zendesk is allowed to send on your behalf, the below must be true:

  • You must add a TXT record (a domain verification record) to your DNS server contains your account's unique domain verification value.
  • The support address is present as an external support address
  • The records were published for longer than the specified TTL (time to live) and successfully propagated

Examining your DNS records

If the above is true, but you are still not able to verify your DNS records, manually inspect your DNS records for errors using a DNS lookup tool. A popular tool third-party tool for this task is MxToolBox. Use the following lookup tool for TXT records:

  • TXT Lookup
Note: The links provided above contain examples of how to correctly lookup your DNS records. Update the example lookup in MxToolBox and replace the value with your actual domain name.

If your records do not appear using the lookup tool above, the records are not properly configured.

Common issues with DNS records

One of the most common issues that occurs when configuring DNS records is that the TXT record is not set up properly.

The TXT record should appear as:

zendeskverification.yourdomain.com

If you are unable to see any of your TXT records using a MxToolBox lookup, reach out to your domain registrar or DNS server provider for further assistance, as they are the only ones able to resolve any propagation issues. If you have an error with an invalid SPF record, see Why does the SPF record fail to verify?

THIS SECTION IS AI CONTENT. DON'T EDIT OR DELETE.

Why do I see the error "DNS records are not set up correctly"? Why does the domain verification fail? What causes the email setup error?

This error appears when Zendesk cannot verify your DNS records. This usually happens because your records are unpublished or published incorrectly.

What are the requirements to allow Zendesk to send email on behalf of my domain? How do I ensure my domain verification records are configured correctly?

To successfully configure your records, you must meet the following requirements:

  1. Add a TXT record containing the unique domain verification value of your account to your DNS server.
  2. Ensure the support address is present as an external support address.
  3. Publish the records for longer than the specified TTL (time to live) to allow successful propagation.

How do I manually check my domain verification records for errors? How do I inspect DNS records with a lookup tool?

You can manually inspect your records using a third-party DNS lookup tool like MxToolBox. Use the TXT Lookup tool, replace the example domain name with your actual domain name, and check if your records appear. If the lookup tool does not display your records, they are not properly configured.

What should the zendeskverification TXT record look like? What is the correct format for the domain verification record?

The TXT record must appear exactly as: zendeskverification.yourdomain.com

What should I do if my TXT records do not appear in a lookup tool? Who resolves DNS propagation issues?

Reach out to your domain registrar or DNS server provider for assistance if you cannot see your TXT records using an MxToolBox lookup. They are the only ones who can resolve propagation issues.

articles.name: Troubleshooting the Web Widget (Classic) widget on your website
articles.title: Troubleshooting the Web Widget (Classic) widget on your website
articles.body:
If the Web Widget (Classic) no longer appears on your website, multiple causes can exist. Use this troubleshooting guide to isolate and fix the issue.

Note: This troubleshooting guide is for those experiencing widget issues on their own website. If your widget is missing from your help center, see Why isn't my Web Widget (Classic) appearing in the Help Center?

Step 1: Verify if the widget operates

To confirm if the widget appears:

  1. Use a tool such as jsfiddle.net to test the widget in isolation and narrow down the issue.
  2. Copy your widget script into the HTML box and click Run.
  3. Your widget pop-up appears in the bottom right-hand side.

If you don't see your widget pop-up, the widget itself doesn't work or isn't configured. Follow steps 3 and 4.

If you see the widget, and confirm the widget itself works, interference or incorrect installation on your website probably causes the problem. Verify steps 2 and 6.

Tip: Clear the cache and cookies of your browser, and test in an incognito or private browsing window.

Step 2: Ensure that the widget is installed correctly

Ensure that these common issues with the widget script don't affect you:

  • The widget script isn't on your website or was removed
  • The wrong widget script appears on your website
  • The correct widget script appears on your website, but not in your header or footer

See How can I find the widget code on my website? to locate and verify that the code is on your website.

If the script isn't present or is incorrect, add it in the header or footer of your source code.

If your widget script is present and correct, ensure you added the script in the header or footer of your website. For more information on how and where the script requires installation, see Adding the Web Widget (Classic) to your website or Help Center.

Step 3: Verify if you have agents online

Verify that at least one agent is online and available to take chats. If you didn't set up a contact form or an offline form, the widget doesn't show up when no agents are online. As a first step, verify these settings and check to see if any agents can receive chats.

For more information, see Setting your chat availability.

Step 4: Check your settings

If you use the Web Widget (Classic), ensure that it appears in Admin Center > Channels > Classic > Web Widget.

If you don't see the widget, add the channel and install the related code on your website.

Step 5: Verify if people are blocked

Check if the visitor who reported the issue isn't allowed in the Chat interface. Country or domain restrictions can also exist for your Chat instance.

If the IP address of the visitor is blocked, the live chat option is suppressed for the user and the widget doesn't appear on the page.

Check that the Require sign-in option isn't selected in your security settings. For more information, see Restricting Help Center access to signed-in end-users. The Web Widget (Classic) only displays the help center content that the user can see. When Require sign-in is selected, the Web Widget (Classic) might not load for non-authenticated users. For more information, see Adding the Web Widget to your website or Help Center.

Step 6: Check for interference and errors

Open your browser console, which can differ depending on your preferred browser. There you can see logs of information such as network requests, CSS, and security errors. Verify if this error appears: ERR_INSECURE_RESPONSE.

If you see that error, you don't currently have an SSL setup. You can tell if you have SSL in place if HTTPS appears at the beginning of your URL. If you don't see HTTPS, you can use a Zendesk Provisioned SSL.

Configure your firewall to allow the web widget to appear.

Note: The Web Widget (Classic) may be blocked if the Firefox setting Enhanced Tracking Protection is turned on. It needs to be turned off for the Web Widget to work.
THIS SECTION IS AI CONTENT. DON'T EDIT OR DELETE.

Why isn't my Web Widget (Classic) appearing on my website? Why is the chat widget missing? How do I fix a hidden website chat popup? How do I troubleshoot the chat widget?

Multiple causes can exist if the Web Widget (Classic) no longer displays on your website. You can isolate and fix the issue by verifying the script installation, checking assignee or agent availability, reviewing channel configurations, checking visitor IP blocks, and inspecting the browser console for network or security interference.

Can I use a tool to test if the Web Widget (Classic) operates? How do I isolate widget issues? Can I test the chat widget code in isolation?

Yes. You can test the widget in isolation to narrow down the problem. Follow these steps:

  1. Use a tool such as jsfiddle.net.
  2. Copy your widget script into the HTML box and click Run.
  3. Verify if your widget pop-up appears in the bottom right-hand side.

If you see the widget, interference or incorrect installation on your website causes the problem. If you do not see the pop-up, the widget itself does not work or lacks proper configuration.

How do I ensure that the Web Widget (Classic) is installed correctly? Where do I place the widget script? What are common widget script installation issues?

Ensure that common script issues do not affect you, such as a missing or removed script, an incorrect script, or placing the code outside the header or footer. Locate and verify the code on your website. If the script is missing or incorrect, add it in the header or footer of your source code.

Does the Web Widget (Classic) disappear when no agents are online? Why does the chat widget hide when support teams are offline?

Yes. If you do not set up a contact form or an offline form, the widget does not show up when no assignees or agents remain online. You must ensure that at least one team member remains online and available to receive chats to keep the live option visible.

Where do I check Web Widget (Classic) settings in the system configuration? How do I add the widget channel in Admin Center?

Ensure that the widget appears in Admin Center > Channels > Classic > Web Widget. If you do not see the widget there, you must add the channel and install the related code on your website.

Does restricting Help Center access to signed-in end users affect the widget? Why is the widget hidden for non-authenticated customers? What happens when "Require sign-in" is turned on?

Yes. The Web Widget (Classic) only displays the Guide or knowledge base content that the requester or customer can see. When you select the Require sign-in option in your security settings, the Web Widget (Classic) might not load for non-authenticated visitors or users.

Why am I seeing the "ERR_INSECURE_RESPONSE" error in the console? Does the Web Widget (Classic) require SSL? How do I fix network security errors for the chat widget?

The "ERR_INSECURE_RESPONSE" error indicates that you do not currently have an SSL setup. You have SSL in place if HTTPS appears at the beginning of your website URL. If you do not see HTTPS, you can use a Zendesk Provisioned SSL to fix the issue. You must also configure your firewall to allow the web widget to appear.

Does the Firefox Enhanced Tracking Protection setting block the Web Widget (Classic)? Can browser privacy settings hide the chat widget?

Yes. The Web Widget (Classic) may be blocked if the Firefox setting Enhanced Tracking Protection is turned on. Requesters or visitors need to turn this setting off for the Web Widget to work properly.

What is a pro tip for troubleshooting widget display issues? Should I clear my browser cache to test the chat widget?

Clear the cache and cookies of your browser, and test the website in an incognito or private browsing window to rule out local browser state issues.

articles.name: My Web Widget (Classic) does not appear on pages outside my help center
articles.title: My Web Widget (Classic) does not appear on pages outside my help center
articles.body:
Issue symptoms

My Web Widget (Classic) does not show on any page other than my help center. My website does not display Chat. This only happens if I selected the Help Center and Contextual Help options in the Web Widget (Classic) settings.

Resolution steps

The Web Widget (Classic) only renders content based on what it has access to. If the widget does not show on a particular website, you most likely ticked the Require sign in option in Knowledge.

When you select that option along with Help Center and Contextual Help in the Web Widget (Classic) settings, Chat cannot load for unauthenticated users. This disables Chat for unauthenticated users, outside the help center.

To resolve this:

  1. Just in case, ensure that the Web Widget (Classic) code appears in the header of your website
  2. In your Web Widget (Classic) settings, enable other options, like Contact form, to restore the Web Widget (Classic) outside your help center
  3. Consider the possibility of unticking Require sign in in Knowledge, which will make your help center content available to anyone
    • You can still restrict the visibility of specific articles and change your article settings in bulk

For more information, see these articles:

  • Why is the contact form missing from Web Widget (Classic)?
  • Troubleshooting the Web Widget (Classic) widget on your website
THIS SECTION IS AI CONTENT. DON'T EDIT OR DELETE.

Why is the Web Widget Classic not showing up on external website pages? Why does the chat bot or answer bot disappear outside the knowledge base or help center? Why is the ticketing system widget missing on my site?

The Web Widget (Classic) only renders content that it has access to. If you select the Help Center and Contextual Help options in your widget settings and also check the Require sign in option in Knowledge, the chat cannot load for unauthenticated visitors or end users outside the help center. This blocks the widget from displaying on external pages.

How do I fix the Web Widget Classic not displaying on pages outside the help center? How do I restore the chat widget or contact form on my website?

You can resolve this display issue by applying these actions:

  1. Verify that the Web Widget (Classic) code appears in the header of your website.
  2. Go to your Web Widget (Classic) settings and enable other options, such as Contact form, to bring back the widget on external pages.
  3. Uncheck the Require sign in option in Knowledge to make your help articles and knowledge base content available to any visitor or customer. You can still restrict the visibility of specific articles or update article settings in bulk.

articles.name: Why does the AI agent provide articles in a different language from the generative reply?
articles.title: Why does the AI agent provide articles in a different language from the generative reply?
articles.body:
Question

Why does the AI agent provide articles in a different language from the generative reply?

Answer

By default, the Web Widget detects the language that the customer's browser sets. You can also set a locale to override the browser language. The AI agent replies based on the language of the customer's message. If the message language differs from the detected locale, the AI agent replies in that language, but the articles beneath appear in the locale that the Web Widget detected.

The AI agent matches the message language, and the related articles follow the Web Widget or browser locale. This keeps content consistent with the interface settings.

For more information, see these articles:

  • How does Zendesk set a language for a user in Support?
  • Creating an AI agent to automatically resolve customer issues
THIS SECTION IS AI CONTENT. DON'T EDIT OR DELETE.

Why does the AI agent or chatbot provide help articles in a different language from the generative reply? Why does the answer bot suggest knowledge base articles in another language? Why do suggested articles mismatch the chatbot reply language?

The AI agent or chatbot replies based on the language of the message from the customer or end user. The related help articles follow the Web Widget or browser locale to keep content consistent with the interface settings. If the message language differs from the detected locale, the generative reply uses the message language while the articles beneath use the detected locale.

Can you override the browser language in the Web Widget? Does the chatbot always use the browser locale for help articles?

By default, the Web Widget detects the language from the browser of the end user or visitor. You can set a specific locale to override the browser language. The AI agent matches the message language for generative replies, but the recommended knowledge base articles follow the detected Web Widget or browser locale.

articles.name: Can I define which customers interact with email AI agents?
articles.title: Can I define which customers interact with email AI agents?
articles.body:
Question

I want to provide higher priority to some of my customers and ensure only human agents address their tickets. Can I define which customers interact with email AI agents?

Answer

Yes. You can use user tags and organization tags to exclude customers from interacting with email advanced AI agents for both Essential AI agents and Advanced AI agents.

Note: If you are part of the Custom Agents EAP, you can also create and designate a custom agent to take action on only customers in your account based on action flows that you define around user or organization tags present in the ticket.

Define which customers interact with email Advanced AI agents

Exclude all customers from an organization

If the customers you want to prioritize and exclusively support with human agents are part of an organization, you can use the organization tag escalated_by_ultimate to restrict their access to email advanced AI agents. You must add this tag to the customers' default organization. New tickets from all users in the organization receive the escalated_by_ultimate tag, which cause the advanced AI agents to skip these tickets.

Exclude single customers

To exclude individual customers, add the tag escalated_by_ultimate to the user's profile. Every ticket from this user will have  include this tag, which the advanced AI agents use to skip the ticket.

Define what customers interact with email Essentials AI agents

When you connect the Essentials AI agent with your email channel, Zendesk creates a trigger named Generative reply for <AI Agent’s name>. This trigger automatically sends generative replies to new tickets.

To define what customers will bypass the AI Agent, add a condition to this trigger that excludes tickets with a unique tag, such as no_ai_reply.

Exclude all customers from an organization

If your customers are part of an organization, use the organization tag no_ai_reply to let them skip the interaction with the AI agent. You must add the tag to the customers' default organization. New tickets from all users in this organization will receive the no_ai_reply tag, which the Essentials AI agents use to identify which tickets to skip.

Exclude single customers

To exclude individual customers, add the tag no_ai_reply to the profile of the user. As a result, every ticket from this user will automatically have this tag, which the essential AI agents use to identify and skip these tickets.

For more information, see Adding tags to users and organizations.

THIS SECTION IS AI CONTENT. DON'T EDIT OR DELETE.

Can I define which customers interact with email AI agents? Can I route specific end users to human agents only? Can I exclude certain companies from chatbot responses? Can I restrict customer accounts from advanced AI agent interactions?

Yes. You can use user tags and organization tags to exclude customers or end users from interacting with email advanced AI agents for both Essential AI agents and Advanced AI agents.

How do I exclude an entire organization or customer account from email Advanced AI agents? How do I route all tickets from a company away from advanced chatbots to support persons?

  1. Navigate to the default organization profile of the company you want to prioritize.
  2. Add the organization tag escalated_by_ultimate to this profile.

New tickets from all users in the organization receive this tag, which causes the advanced AI agents to skip these tickets.

How do I exclude a single customer or end user from email Advanced AI agents? How do I stop advanced chatbots from answering an individual requester?

  1. Open the specific user profile of the customer.
  2. Add the tag escalated_by_ultimate directly to the user's profile.

Every ticket from this user includes this tag, which the advanced AI agents use to skip the ticket.

How do I define what customers interact with email Essentials AI agents? How do I bypass the auto-reply or notification rule for specific requesters?

  1. Locate the trigger named Generative reply for <AI Agent's name> that Zendesk creates when you connect the Essentials AI agent with your email channel.
  2. Add a condition to this trigger that excludes tickets with a unique tag, such as no_ai_reply.

This configuration ensures that tickets matching the exclusion tag bypass the generative replies automated action.

How do I exclude an entire organization from email Essentials AI agents? How do I stop the Essentials chatbot from auto-replying to a company account?

  1. Go to the default organization profile for the company.
  2. Add the organization tag no_ai_reply to the profile.

New tickets from all users in this organization receive the tag, which the Essentials AI agents use to identify which tickets to skip.

How do I exclude a single customer from email Essentials AI agents? How do I block the Essentials answer bot from responding to an individual end user?

  1. Open the user profile of the individual customer.
  2. Add the tag no_ai_reply to the profile of the user.

Every ticket from this user automatically carries this tag, which the essential AI agents use to identify and skip these tickets.

What features do I get if I am part of the Custom Agents EAP? Can I create custom action flows for user or organization tags in the EAP?

If you participate in the Custom Agents EAP, you can create and designate a custom agent to take action on only customers in your account based on action flows that you define around user or organization tags present in the ticket.

articles.name: Why can't I create reports or dashboards in Zendesk Explore?
articles.title: Why can't I create reports or dashboards in Zendesk Explore?
articles.body:
Question

Why can't I create reports or dashboards in Explore?

Answer

You might not be able to create reports or dashboards due to plan limitations or permission issues.

Plan limitations

Zendesk Explore offers different plans with different features. Explore Lite is available with standalone Zendesk Support plans, such as Support Team, Support Professional, and Support Enterprise. Explore Lite agents can only access prebuilt dashboards created by Zendesk. Explore Lite plans don't permit users to create custom reports or dashboards.

If your current Explore plan is Explore Lite and you need advanced capabilities, consider an upgrade to your Explore plan.

See Check your plan type or add-ons.

Permission issues

If you have the right subscription plan and still can't create custom reports and dashboards, it might be a user permission issue.

An admin can confirm if your user role includes the necessary permissions to build reports and dashboards.

See Give users access to Explore.

THIS SECTION IS AI CONTENT. DON'T EDIT OR DELETE.

Why can't I create reports or dashboards in Explore? Can I build custom reporting or analytics dashboards? Why is the option to make custom reports unavailable in my help desk?

You cannot create custom reports or dashboards if you have subscription plan limitations or user permission issues. Explore Lite plans do not permit you to create custom reports or dashboards. If you have the correct pricing tier but still cannot build reports, your user role might lack the necessary permissions.

What features does Explore Lite offer for reporting and analytics? Can I customize dashboards on an Explore Lite pricing tier? What access do agents have on the Lite suite version?

Explore Lite provides access only to prebuilt dashboards created by Zendesk. Explore Lite stands as the service level available with standalone Support plans, including Support Team, Support Professional, and Support Enterprise. This plan type does not let you build custom metrics, reports, or dashboards.

articles.name: Configuring automatic call acceptance for agents in Contact Center
articles.title: Configuring automatic call acceptance for agents in Contact Center
articles.body:
Admins can configure Contact Center so that inbound calls are automatically accepted on behalf of agents. This setting is controlled on a per-agent basis in Amazon Connect Customer, so you can enable auto-accept for some agents and leave it disabled for others.

What's my plan?
Add-on Contact Center

Summary: ◀▼

You can turn on automatic call acceptance for inbound voice contacts so selected agents’ calls are accepted for them. This per-agent setting helps agents handle calls with less clicking, while still letting you enable it only for the users you want. You can also update auto-accept later with bulk edit after users are created.

Admins can configure Contact Center so that inbound calls are automatically accepted on behalf of agents. This setting is controlled on a per-agent basis in Amazon Connect Customer, so you can enable auto-accept for some agents and leave it disabled for others.

When an incoming voice contact reaches an available agent with auto-accept enabled, the contact console may briefly display the call notification before the call is automatically accepted. The call console displays only an Accept option and Show details for any call information (for example, contact attributes). For more information, see Automatically accepting calls.

You can’t configure per-channel auto-accept during user creation when importing users with a .csv template. Instead, create the users first, then use Bulk Edit to update their per-channel auto-accept settings.

To configure calls auto-accept

  1. Access your Amazon Connect instance, see Accessing Amazon Connect and AWS Console from Contact Center.
  2. Under Users, click Users.
  3. In the list of users, select one or more agents, then click Edit.
  4. Under Settings > Contact handling click the Auto-accept contacts toggle to turn automatic acceptance for Voice on.
  5. Click Save.

articles.name: Service Incident - July 9, 2026 - Support | All Pods - Side Conversation PDF Attachments Failing to Load
articles.title: Service Incident - July 9, 2026 - Support | All Pods - Side Conversation PDF Attachments Failing to Load
articles.body:
Summary

On July 9, 2026, from 11:59 UTC to 16:49 UTC, some customers were unable to open PDF attachments from Side Conversations in Agent Workspace, and saw the error message “Failed to load PDF attachment.”

Timeline


July 09, 2026 12:13 PM 
Between 11:59 - 16:49 UTC on July 9th some customers experienced issues accessing attachments on side conversations, resulting in the error message “Failed to load PDF attachment”. We have since reverted the change and attachments are now accessible as expected. We apologize for the disruption this caused.

Customer impact

  • Who was affected: Some Support customers using Side Conversations in Agent Workspace (across multiple pods).
  • What happened: The PDF viewer could spin and then fail with “Failed to load PDF attachment.”
  • When: Approximately 11:59 UTC to 16:49 UTC on July 9, 2026.
  • What was not impacted: Other (non-PDF) attachments continued to work as expected.
  • Data loss / exposure: None.

Root cause

A change deployed to our Side Conversations attachment handling introduced an unintended behavior change for PDF downloads.

Specifically, part of the update that was intended to be controlled behind a feature flag was not fully gated, so the change affected more traffic than intended. That caused the PDF download/streaming flow to break in production, resulting in PDFs failing to load in the Agent Workspace viewer.

Mitigation and recovery

We reverted the change at 16:49 UTC, which restored normal PDF attachment loading.

What we’re doing to prevent this in the future

  • Ensure the full PDF-handling change is completely gated behind the feature flag (so behavior changes are atomic and can’t partially apply).
  • Add automated test coverage to validate both “flag on” and “flag off” behavior.
  • Strengthen pre-production validation to test the full end-to-end behavior in staging for both flag states.
  • Improve monitoring/alerting on the attachment download path so regressions are detected faster.

FOR MORE INFORMATION

For current system status information about Zendesk and specific impacts to your account, visit our system status page. If you have additional questions about this incident, contact Zendesk customer support.

articles.name: My AI agent is not recommending articles to users who are authenticated with JWT
articles.title: My AI agent is not recommending articles to users who are authenticated with JWT
articles.body:
Issue symptoms

I noticed that authenticated users are unable to see recommended articles in messaging. However, article recommendations are enabled in the AI agent messaging behavior.

Resolution steps

End users who are authenticated with JWT can only access articles when their user profile in Zendesk is created before their conversation starts with the AI agent.

For this to happen, ensure the end user exists in your Zendesk database before the user interacts with the Web Widget. This creates the external ID used to check the user profile and their access permissions. User profiles without an external ID cannot see article recommendations, even if they are authenticated with JWT.

For more information, see this article: What are the different types of JWT proposed by Zendesk?

This article describes functionality available only to customers who had a drafted or published AI agent as of February 2, 2025. For information about equivalent functionality in the AI agents - Advanced add-on.
 
THIS SECTION IS AI CONTENT. DON'T EDIT OR DELETE.

Why are authenticated users not seeing recommended articles in messaging? Why is my AI agent or chatbot not recommending help articles to JWT authenticated customers? Why are article recommendations missing for verified end users in the Web Widget?

End users who authenticate with JWT can only see recommended articles if their user profile in Zendesk exists before their conversation starts with the AI agent. If you do not create the customer profile in your Zendesk database prior to the interaction, the system cannot verify the external ID used to check profiles and access permissions.

How do I fix missing article recommendations for JWT authenticated users in messaging? How do I ensure authenticated customers see help articles from the Answer bot?

  1. Create the end user profile in your Zendesk database before the customer interacts with the Web Widget.
  2. Ensure this action generates the external ID needed to check the user profile and permissions.

What are the limitations of article recommendations for JWT authenticated users? Which pricing tiers or product setups support AI agent article recommendations for authenticated customers?

User profiles without an external ID cannot see article recommendations, even when authenticated with JWT and when you enable article recommendations in the AI agent messaging behavior. This specific functionality is available only to customers who had a drafted or published AI agent as of February 2, 2025.

articles.name: How can I reconnect Zendesk QA?
articles.title: How can I reconnect Zendesk QA?
articles.body:
Question

Some of my tickets do not import into Zendesk QA. What can I do?

Answer

If tickets do not appear in Zendesk QA, the connection to your help desk likely expired. This usually happens after a password change, an admin change, or a bulk edit in your help desk. Zendesk QA pauses the import of new conversations until you renew the connection.

To access Connections:

  1. Select the Settings icon (your profile photo)
  2. Select the Toggle sidebar icon
  3. Under Account, select Connections
  4. Check whether the connection status is Active or Inactive
  5. If the connection is inactive, select the icon with three dots on the right, then select Renew connection
Note: The Renew connection button is only available for Zendesk QA accounts that did not migrate from Klaus.
 
THIS SECTION IS AI CONTENT. DON'T EDIT OR DELETE.

Why do some tickets not import into Zendesk QA? Why do tickets stop appearing or sync pause? Why are new conversations missing in my QA tool?

Tickets do not import when the connection to your help desk or ticketing system expires. This connection drop usually happens after a password change, an admin or support person change, or a bulk edit in your help desk. Zendesk QA pauses the import of new conversations automatically until you renew the connection.

How do I reconnect Zendesk QA? How do I renew my help desk connection? How do I fix an inactive connection?

You can restore your connection through the settings menu. Follow these steps to reconnect:

  1. Select the Settings icon, which displays as your profile photo.
  2. Select the Toggle sidebar icon.
  3. Under Account, select Connections.
  4. Check whether the connection status displays as Active or Inactive.
  5. If the connection status is inactive, select the icon with three dots on the right side, then select Renew connection.

Can I renew my connection if my account migrated from Klaus? Is the Renew connection button available for migrated accounts?

No. You can only use the Renew connection button if your Zendesk QA account did not migrate from Klaus.

articles.name: Why can't I see the full bot conversation at the start of a messaging ticket?
articles.title: Why can't I see the full bot conversation at the start of a messaging ticket?
articles.body:
Question

When a new messaging ticket arrives, I don't see the full bot conversation the user went through. Why doesn't the ticket include all the bot interactions?

Answer

You can see the initial bot interaction for 72 hours after the user's last message. After 72 hours without activity, the system clears that interaction and restarts it when the user returns to the bot.

If a user follows the flow and stays inactive for more than 72 hours, the next user message resets the conversation and resumes from where the user left off in the flow. If that user transfers to an agent, the system creates a new messaging ticket, and you do not see the earlier bot interaction history from before that inactivity period.

If a user starts a bot conversation and remains active in the Web Widget and sends messages within 72 hours, you see the full conversation history in the ticket.

Tip: When a messaging conversation approaches inactivity, Zendesk can send inactivity reminders to the user. Admins can now add tags to those reminder tickets to help track inactive conversations, identify follow-ups, and build reporting workflows around them. For more information, see this article: Sending conversation inactivity reminders to end users

For an entire list of messaging resources, see Web and mobile messaging resources.

THIS SECTION IS AI CONTENT. DON'T EDIT OR DELETE.

Why can't I see the full chatbot conversation history at the start of a messaging ticket? Why does a messaging ticket clear the AI agent interaction history? Why is the chat history missing when an end user transfers to a team member?

You only see the initial chatbot conversation history for 72 hours after the customer sends their last message. After 72 hours of inactivity, the system clears the interaction and resets the history when the customer returns to the AI agent.

Can I see the full chat history if the customer remains active? Does Zendesk preserve the chatbot interaction history when messages occur within 72 hours? Will the messaging ticket show the entire conversation if the end user stays active?

Yes. If the customer starts a chatbot conversation, remains active in the Web Widget, and sends messages within 72 hours, you see the full conversation history in the messaging ticket.

What are the time limits for viewing AI agent conversations in a messaging ticket? What happens to the chat history after 72 hours of inactivity? When does Zendesk reset the conversation history and create a new messaging ticket?

The system enforces a 72-hour inactivity limit. If a customer remains inactive for more than 72 hours and then resumes the flow, the next message resets the conversation. If that customer transfers to a team member, the system creates a new messaging ticket and removes the earlier chatbot interaction history.

How can I track inactive messaging conversations? Can admins add tags to conversation inactivity reminders? How do I build reporting workflows for inactive tickets?

Admins can add tags to conversation inactivity reminders sent to customers. Use these tags to track inactive conversations, identify follow-ups, and build reporting workflows.

Can I display all my personal ticket views? Why are some personal views missing when I go to the Views icon? Is it possible to show all personal views on the page? Can I see every custom list of tickets I created?

You can display 10 personal views and 100 shared views. If you hit the limit with your personal views, work with your administrator to create shared views for you and other agents.

articles.name: How does Zendesk send email notifications?
articles.title: How does Zendesk send email notifications?
articles.body:
Question

How do I send an email? Are emails sent as soon as I submit a ticket?

Answer

Before Zendesk sends an email notification, you must meet the delivery prerequisites. You must have at least one verified email address. You must verify DNS records. The recipient must have an active email address.

Email notifications use a multi-layered system. Business rules, such as triggers and automations, determine when notifications are sent.

Triggers fire immediately when conditions are met. Triggers execute in real time.

By default, the system includes triggers to send notifications to customers for these events:

  • When their new request is received
  • When an agent submits a public comment

The default triggers also notify agents of these events:

  • When a new request is received
  • When the agent is assigned a ticket
  • When a request is assigned to the group of an agent
  • When an end user submits a comment to an assigned ticket

Automations can also notify customers. Apart from the Request customer satisfaction rating (system automation), no other default automation sends emails to customers. Automations run once per hour and check time-based conditions.

Each trigger or automation references an email template:

  • Templates define content and appearance
  • Templates use placeholders: {{ticket.title}}, {{ticket.description}}
  • You can customize templates in Admin Center > Channels > Email > Templates

Alternatively, if the plan has access to Advanced AI Agents, an AI agent for email can configure a generative reply in an email from a messaging conversation instead. For more information, see About the Generative replies block in conversation flows for advanced AI agents.

To check which which action sent the notification email on a ticket based on your setup, check the Events view of the ticket for any Email notification event.

Note: Email conversation flows are available by default for any new email in advanced AI agents.
THIS SECTION IS AI CONTENT. DON'T EDIT OR DELETE.

What is the email notification system? How does Zendesk send email notifications? What determines when notification rules or auto-replies go out?

Email notifications use a multi-layered system. Business rules, such as triggers or auto-replies and automations or time-based rules, determine when notifications go out.

Can I send an email notification immediately? Are emails sent as soon as I submit a ticket? Do triggers or auto-replies execute in real time?

Yes. Triggers or auto-replies fire immediately when conditions are met. These rules execute in real time.

What are the prerequisites for email delivery? What limits or conditions apply before Zendesk sends an email notification? What do I need to verify to send notifications to a customer or end user?

You must meet specific delivery prerequisites before the system sends a notification. You must have at least one verified email address and you must verify DNS records. Additionally, the recipient or customer must have an active email address.

How do I customize templates? Where do I edit email templates in the Admin Center?

You can customize the content and appearance of templates by navigating to Admin Center Channels Email Templates.

Can I use AI agents or chatbots to reply to emails? How do advanced AI agents configure generative replies?

Yes. If your plan type or pricing tier has access to Advanced AI Agents or chatbots, an AI agent for email can configure a generative reply in an email from a messaging conversation instead. Email conversation flows are available by default for any new email in advanced AI agents.

How do I check which action sent the notification email on a ticket? How do I find the email notification event?

You can check the specific action that sent the notification email by performing the following steps:

  1. Open the ticket.
  2. Open the Events view of the ticket.
  3. Look for any Email notification event.

articles.name: How do I export a list of users?
articles.title: How do I export a list of users?
articles.body:
Question

I want to get a list of users in Zendesk Support. How can I download my user information to share outside of my account?

Answer

Use basic data export

Basic data export functionality is available to these Support and Suite plans. An account admin or custom role with export permissions can export a list of users in the XML or JSON format. Agents can only export users with the API. Download the file and manually filter it with software like Excel or Google Sheets.

For Enterprise accounts, admins can use a granular permission to restrict CSV exports. If you use a custom role and cannot see the export options, ensure your admin has enabled the CSV Export permission for your role.

Verify your plan against these plan requirements. If your plan allows exports, yet you do not see the Reports option, contact Zendesk Customer Support to request the feature.

Use an add-on

Accounts that purchased the customer list add-on can export user lists in a CSV file. This feature targets groups of users and includes the information of your choice in an export. Use the customer list add-on to export enhanced user list views, customer segmentation, or to schedule exports.

  • Enterprise Plans: Exporting CSV files from customer lists requires a custom role with the CSV Export permission enabled
  • Contributor Role: The Contributor role is a viewer-only role and does not support CSV exports. To allow an agent to export lists, they must be assigned to a Light Agent role or a custom role with export permissions.

Use the API

The REST API Users endpoint is available to all Zendesk accounts. The API gives you flexibility and control, but requires the assistance of someone with basic knowledge of code. If you have developer resources on your team, the API is a good option.

Use the Marketplace

Explore third-party alternatives to install from the Zendesk Marketplace, for example, the Advanced Search Plus app. In that app in Support, use the * search parameter to return all Users.

This article is provided for instructional purposes only. Zendesk cannot provide support for third-party apps. Reach out to the email address of the developer under App details, from the app page in the Marketplace, or search for a solution online.
THIS SECTION IS AI CONTENT. DON'T EDIT OR DELETE.

How do I export a list of users? How can I download my user information or customer data to share outside of my account? How do I extract my assignee or end user records?

You can export a list of users by using the basic data export functionality, purchasing the customer list add-on, utilizing the REST API, or installing a third-party alternative app from the Zendesk Marketplace.

Can I download user data as a CSV file? Can agents export user lists? Can a read-only agent export customer information?

Accounts with the customer list add-on can export user lists in a CSV file. Agents can only export users with the API. The Contributor role is a viewer-only role and does not support CSV exports; you must assign agents to a Light Agent role or a custom role with export permissions to allow exports.

What formats does the basic data export support? What permissions do I need to export data? Why can I not see the data export options?

The basic data export supports XML or JSON formats. An account admin or a custom role with export permissions can export the list. For Enterprise accounts, ensure your admin enables the CSV Export permission for your custom role. If your plan allows exports but you do not see the Reports option, contact Zendesk Customer Support.

What is the customer list add-on? Why should I use the customer list add-on or paid plugin?

The customer list add-on is a feature that targets groups of users and includes the information of your choice in a CSV export. Use this add-on to export enhanced user list views, perform customer segmentation, or schedule exports.

How do I use the API to export users? How do I use the Zendesk Marketplace to download customer data?

  1. Access the REST API Users endpoint, which is available to all Zendesk accounts and requires basic knowledge of code.
  2. Explore third-party alternatives to install from the Zendesk Marketplace, such as the Advanced Search Plus app.
  3. Use the * search parameter in the app within Support to return all Users.
